title: Stepan Paul
text:
I am an Associate Teaching Professor of Mathematics at North Carolina State University where I’ve worked since 2020. I focus on teaching, curriculum development, mathematical illustration, and service to the undergraduate program in the department.
I am the Adminstrator for the Minor in Mathematics at NC State, and I coordinate MA 242 - Calculus III (multivariable and vector calculus) and MA 103 - Topics in Contemporary Mathematics (a liberal arts mathematics course). I also organize the departmental Teaching and Learning Seminar.
I serve on the Departmental Faculty Advisor Committee, Mathematics Undergraduate Course and Curriculum Committee, Recruitment Committee, and Undergraduate Advising Committee.

Tangent Developable of a Twisted Cubic



Illustration



Visulaizations of the tangent developable of a twisted cubic curve illustrating its ruling and flat metric.



Stepan Paul and Adrien Abgrall

Spindle Earth



Illustration



A visualization of the spindle earth, a surface of revolution with constant positive curvature and two singular points.



Stepan Paul and Steve Trettel
